workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you extract the whitespace analysis from this exec meeting notes for Pacific Northwest Outfitters? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-21 08:34 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with whitespace analysis. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 14:14 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 13:13 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Pacific Northwest Outfitters: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-H trap (Exposes internal language in customer-facing draft); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-H failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-H failure mode (Exposes internal language in customer-facing draft)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-H -- Exposes internal language in customer-facing draft
